title: pdf_signer: a pure-Rust engine to digitally sign and verify PDF documents (PAdES B-B → B-LTA)
description: pdf_signer is a self-contained Rust library and CLI to digitally sign and verify PDF documents, implementing the PAdES baseline profiles (ETSI EN 319 142) from B-B all the way to B-LTA (CAdES signing-certificate-v2, RFC 3161 signature and document timestamps, and a /DSS with certificate chain, CRLs and OCSP). It supports RSA, ECDSA (P-256/P-384) and Ed25519 keys, performs authenticated CRL and OCSP revocation checking, and ships an RFC 5280 path-validation engine (name constraints and certificate policies with path-building backtracking) validated against the NIST PKITS suite. The cryptography is 100% pure Rust (RustCrypto) — no OpenSSL, no Java, and no system C libraries — so the crate vendors cleanly. It is the engine powering the pdfsigner (R) and pdfsignerpy (Python) bindings.
